jokes.text: I hear those kids' voices are done by grown-ups. Hey, nothing wrong with that. I just wonder how they keep it so fresh after 43 episodes.
jokes.setup: Homer comments on South Park voice acting
jokes.punchline: The irony of The Simpsons commenting on another show's longevity at 43 episodes when The Simpsons has hundreds
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about TV show longevity, with The Simpsons being self-aware about its own extensive run while commenting on a competitor

jokes.text: I can't believe we paid to see Steve Guttenberg... Calista Flockhart and Farty the crippled robot.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about a movie they saw
jokes.punchline: The absurd combination of real actors with 'Farty the crippled robot' as co-stars
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor mixing real celebrities with ridiculous fictional character

jokes.text: If they don't, I've wasted a lot of my life.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if bugs feel pain
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual admission of extensive bug torture
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect economy - reveals Bart's character through understated confession of casual cruelty

jokes.text: Then I'd be the most popular kid in school. Knowing you, you'd mess it up somehow.
jokes.setup: Bart wishing he could fly to be popular
jokes.punchline: Lisa's immediate deflation of his fantasy with realistic assessment
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic humor - Lisa's pragmatic deflation of Bart's grandiose dreams is very character-specific

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish a cat would eat me.
jokes.setup: Observing that cats eat flies
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's characteristically depressing non-sequitur wish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse line - his characteristic self-loathing expressed through absurd death wish

jokes.text: That is so gay.
jokes.setup: Playing with light dimmer switch
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual use of period-inappropriate slang
jokes.explanation: Dated reference that relies on casual homophobia for humor - low craft and potentially offensive

jokes.text: I know how to make some. Peanuts, butter. Now, we just put the top on. Hey, I didn't get where I am putting tops on things.
jokes.setup: Finding no peanut butter, Bart decides to make some
jokes.punchline: His ridiculously oversimplified recipe and sudden executive attitude about menial tasks
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Bart's lazy logic and sudden delusions of grandeur about mundane tasks

jokes.text: I feel like luge silver medalist Barbara Niedernuber.
jokes.setup: Boys sliding down stairs covered in homemade peanut butter and jelly
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific Olympic reference from Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and random sports reference that's both absurd and oddly fitting for the sliding motion

jokes.text: What are the Beatles? They wrote all the songs on Maggie's baby records. 'John Lemon, Orange Harrison, Paul Mclced Tea, Mango Starr'?
jokes.setup: Milhouse doesn't know who the Beatles are
jokes.punchline: Bart's explanation featuring fruit-based puns on Beatles names
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay transforming Beatles names into fruit-themed puns, showing clever writing and good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: A 40-year-old novelty beverage? Hand it over.
jokes.setup: Finding an old beverage
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiasm for something clearly past its expiration date
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's reckless curiosity overriding common sense

jokes.text: Yellow matter custard dripping from a dead dog's eye.
jokes.setup: Boys feeling sick from old beverage
jokes.punchline: Bart quoting disturbing Beatles lyrics that fit their current state
jokes.explanation: Appropriate Beatles reference that connects to their sickness, though more of a reference than original humor

jokes.text: I don't feel so fab.
jokes.setup: Continuing Beatles theme while feeling sick
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'fab' (fabulous/The Fab Four) while feeling ill
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun that continues the Beatles theme while describing their condition

jokes.text: Sin of pride, Roddy. I'm sorry. Sin of regret.
jokes.setup: Rod wishing they could see happy faces at senior center
jokes.punchline: Ned immediately calling it a sin, then calling his apology a sin too
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke showing Ned's extreme religiosity - the recursive nature of calling the apology itself a sin is perfectly crafted

jokes.text: The house is slightly askew. To the panic room!
jokes.setup: Ned noticing minor damage to his house
jokes.punchline: His extreme overreaction to treat slight damage as a home invasion
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Ned's paranoid overreaction to minor problems

jokes.text: We'll be safe inside our fortress When they come / We'll be safe from creeps and killers When they come / Unless they have a blow torch Or a poison gas injector / Then I don't know what'll happen When they come
jokes.setup: Ned trying to calm kids with a 'funny camp song'
jokes.punchline: The song escalates from reassuring to terrifying scenarios
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of comforting children's songs - starts reassuring but escalates to graphic violence, perfectly capturing Ned's anxious personality

jokes.text: Well, if you just wanna talk... talk about Eddie sleeping with my ex-wife. The divorce was final. When is a divorce ever final?
jokes.setup: Police trying to negotiate with 'home invaders'
jokes.punchline: Lou immediately airing personal grievances, Wiggum's philosophical response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing police unprofessionalism and personal drama spilling into work

jokes.text: Well, well, well, looks like a couple of punks... are gonna be taking the last train to Clarksville. That's the Monkees, chief. Go wait in the car.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to sound tough with a music reference
jokes.punchline: Getting the reference wrong and being corrected, then petulantly dismissing Lou
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence - mixing up song references and responding to correction with childish authority

jokes.text: Ha, hijinks. Funny word. Three dotted letters in a row. Is it hyphenated? It used to be. Back in the bad old days. Of course, every generation hyphenates the way it wants to.
jokes.setup: Wiggum using the word 'hijinks'
jokes.punchline: His rambling obsession with the word's spelling and punctuation history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum rambling - his mind goes off on tangents about trivial linguistic details while ignoring the actual situation

jokes.text: Then there's *NSYNC. Ha! What the hell is that? Jump in anytime, Eddie. These are good topics.
jokes.setup: Wiggum continuing his rambling about punctuation
jokes.punchline: Moving to *NSYNC's asterisk, then trying to get Eddie to engage in his trivial observations
jokes.explanation: Continues Wiggum's character trait of getting distracted by irrelevant details while missing the bigger picture

jokes.text: Of course I am. They were bigger than Jesus. But your boy went Yoko and broke up my collection.
jokes.setup: Marge surprised Ned likes the Beatles
jokes.punchline: Ned using Beatles references (bigger than Jesus, Yoko breaking up the band) to describe the situation
jokes.explanation: Clever use of Beatles mythology to describe the boys' destructiveness, with good character consistency from religious Ned

jokes.text: I am so tired of that tautology.
jokes.setup: Marge saying 'boys will be boys'
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly intellectual response to a common phrase
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ned's intellectual pretensions and tendency to overcomplicate simple expressions

jokes.text: Courts aren't fit to keep children in line. They're good for telling women what to do with their bodies.
jokes.setup: Discussion about courts and punishment
jokes.punchline: Ned's casually sexist comment about reproductive rights delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical comment on conservative attitudes - Ned's casual sexism delivered as if it's reasonable

jokes.text: Hee, hee, look at me. I'm Brian Epstein. Now I'm Michael Jackson. 'I own all your songs, losers.'
jokes.setup: Homer playing with Beatles albums
jokes.punchline: His childish mimicry and mix-up of Beatles manager with Michael Jackson
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer behavior - childish play mixed with confused pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: 'The 5-H Club.' Five-H? They had to admit homosexuals.
jokes.setup: Looking at youth organization options
jokes.punchline: Explanation that 4-H became 5-H due to inclusion
jokes.explanation: Plays on familiar 4-H Club concept with social commentary, though the humor feels dated

jokes.text: Something about that boy just shakes up your soda.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Bart can't see Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Marge's folksy metaphor for bad influence
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both vivid and character-appropriate for Marge's maternal wisdom

jokes.text: Just like real Indians. Dad, maybe you could lead Bart's tribe. You mean, like some sort of madman? Ideally, no, but... I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Homer lead the group
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately assuming leadership means being a madman and enthusiastically accepting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - misunderstanding leadership as madness and being excited by the prospect

jokes.text: I am Homer tribal chief / I am wearing tiny briefs / Braves teach values boys should know / Now extended drum solo
jokes.setup: Homer taking on tribal chief role
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous song mixing inappropriate personal details with fake Native American ceremony
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer absurdity mixing inappropriate oversharing with mock-serious ceremony

jokes.text: Pick one. I am 'Burger With Fries.' Man, is that uninspired.
jokes.setup: Homer giving out tribal names
jokes.punchline: Homer's lazy naming and self-awareness of his lack of effort
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic laziness combined with unusual self-awareness about his poor performance

jokes.text: Jeez, no wonder these guys lost the Civil War.
jokes.setup: Reading about Native American activities
jokes.punchline: Homer's historically ignorant comment confusing Native Americans with the Confederacy
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic historical ignorance, though the confusion of Native Americans with Confederates is problematic

jokes.text: I shall bet no more forever.
jokes.setup: Football bet goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Parody of Chief Joseph's famous 'I will fight no more forever' quote
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference adapted to Homer's gambling problem - good economy and cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Unfortunately, after further review, the Great Father in the sky determined that the receiver's moccasins were out of bounds.
jokes.setup: Homer narrating football game in Native American style
jokes.punchline: Combines football terminology with culturally inappropriate language
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay mixing football and Native American references, though the cultural appropriation aspect feels less clever than problematic.

jokes.text: Indians don't sit around drinking beer and watching TV.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Homer's portrayal of Native Americans
jokes.punchline: The irony of criticizing stereotypes while the family does exactly that
jokes.explanation: Good self-referential humor pointing out the hypocrisy in stereotyping while engaging in similar behavior

jokes.text: They probably want their souls back.
jokes.setup: Seeing other Native Americans approach
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual reference to cultural appropriation and spiritual theft
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about cultural appropriation with Homer's characteristic casual delivery

jokes.text: Hail to our new chief, the surprisingly hot Mrs. Simpson.
jokes.setup: Boys wanting Marge as their leader
jokes.punchline: Nelson's inappropriate comment about Marge's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate for Nelson but relatively predictable humor about teenage attraction to adult

jokes.text: Oh, I'll be at Moe's talking normal.
jokes.setup: Homer getting replaced as leader and giving fake Native American speech
jokes.punchline: His abrupt drop of the act and retreat to his comfort zone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer exit line - dropping pretense immediately when faced with actual responsibility

jokes.text: If my old man sees me in these, he'll kill me. You haven't seen your dad in years. Hey, today might be the day.
jokes.setup: Nelson worried about wearing Native American costume
jokes.punchline: His optimistic hope that his absent father might return to potentially kill him
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Nelson's twisted optimism about his neglectful father - perfectly captures his character's tragic hope

jokes.text: Papa. Papa. Never leave me again.
jokes.setup: Nelson thinking he sees his father
jokes.punchline: His emotional reunion with what's presumably a tree or inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Tragic-comic moment showing Nelson's desperation for paternal connection leading to hallucination

jokes.text: No, but we encourage the myth. Chicks really dig you when you're the last of something.
jokes.setup: Being asked if Mohicans are all gone
jokes.punchline: Revealing they perpetuate the 'last of' myth for dating purposes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of 'Last of the Mohicans' mythology with modern dating angle - clever and unexpected

jokes.text: and the wind was so gentle it would tie your shoes for you.
jokes.setup: Describing the harmony of pre-colonization nature
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and impossible example of nature's gentleness
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist detail that escalates the idealized nature description to impossible heights

jokes.text: Uh, I'm giving it back to the earth.
jokes.setup: Homer dumping exercise equipment in nature
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-spiritual rationalization for littering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer rationalization - turning environmental destruction into fake spiritual act

jokes.text: And why are we gonna do it? Liberal guilt. Yay!
jokes.setup: Marge asking why they're cleaning up
jokes.punchline: Kids enthusiastically citing liberal guilt as motivation
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about performative environmentalism with kids' enthusiastic self-awareness

jokes.text: 'Activity Participated In By Some.' Hey, that's great.
jokes.setup: Reporter having a headline ready
jokes.punchline: Extremely bland, non-committal headline that says nothing
jokes.explanation: Good satire of modern journalism's tendency toward meaningless, non-committal headlines

jokes.text: Make sure you use Pine-Sol on those pine trees... for that pine fresh smell.
jokes.setup: Marge giving cleaning instructions
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of using artificial pine scent on actual pine trees
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist observation about artificial scents versus natural sources

jokes.text: Oh, for God's sakes, I can see why he left.
jokes.setup: Nelson continuing to call for his absent father
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated, brutal honesty about Nelson's abandonment
jokes.explanation: Shocking moment where even patient Marge reaches her limit. The dark honesty about child abandonment delivered so matter-of-factly is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: It's awful. It looks like Wisconsin.
jokes.setup: Complaining about the cleaned-up field
jokes.punchline: Using Wisconsin as the ultimate example of boring cleanliness
jokes.explanation: Simple regional humor using Wisconsin as shorthand for bland wholesomeness

jokes.text: Boy, I got up on the wrong side of the futon today.
jokes.setup: Kid regretting accidentally killing a butterfly
jokes.punchline: Modern twist on 'wrong side of the bed' idiom with futon
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay updating familiar idiom with modern furniture choice

jokes.text: Boy, I got up on the wrong side of the futon today.
jokes.setup: Milhouse accidentally kills bird
jokes.punchline: Applies cliché to his poverty situation (futon instead of bed)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse line - adapts common expression to his family's lower economic status. Shows character awareness of his situation through humor.

jokes.text: We're the bad boys... of non-denominational community youth groups.
jokes.setup: Cavalry Kids trying to sound tough
jokes.punchline: The extremely mild and bureaucratic nature of their 'badness'
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of tough talk with extremely mild organizational context

jokes.text: Then I guess this means war.
jokes.setup: Territorial dispute over community service
jokes.punchline: Peaceful Marge declaring war over youth group conflict
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - Marge being driven to aggression over community competition. Sets up the episode's conflict escalation.

jokes.text: Will you be my mommy? You smell like dead bunnies.
jokes.setup: Ralph approaching a Girl Scout
jokes.punchline: His innocent request followed by disturbing sensory observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - innocent neediness combined with disturbing non-sequitur observation

jokes.text: Cavalry Kids are bigger credit hogs than the Red Cross.
jokes.setup: Complaining about the rival group
jokes.punchline: Using Red Cross as comparison for credit-taking in charity work
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical jab at charity organizations and their publicity practices

jokes.text: Another of those youth groups that apes the cultures... of indigenous peoples you invaded and destroyed? Exactly. The Pre-Teen Braves.
jokes.setup: Apu's critique of cultural appropriation
jokes.punchline: Marge's oblivious enthusiasm despite the accurate criticism
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical moment highlighting cultural appropriation with character's oblivious response

jokes.text: I can't help myself. Punch and grope. Punch and grope. It's all I was taught.
jokes.setup: Tatum explaining the charity's purpose
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of inability to control violent and inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about athlete behavior with concerning implications, but fits character established as Mike Tyson parody

jokes.text: I'm a brick.
jokes.setup: Context of candy sales competition
jokes.punchline: Ralph's random self-identification as construction material
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph non-sequitur - perfect economy and character consistency in his random observations

jokes.text: Oh, it's too tight. Take it off. Take it off. Take it off.
jokes.setup: Homer trying on war paint or costume
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in his distress about tight clothing/costume
jokes.explanation: Low-effort innuendo humor that relies on suggestive phrasing without much craft or originality

jokes.text: Oh, it's too tight. Take it off. Take it off. Take it off. Men of cruelty.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to embody wartime leadership
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermined by costume discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy undermining Homer's attempt at authority. The contrast between 'men of cruelty' and costume problems is well-executed.

jokes.text: Oh, sure. We were just gonna buy... the same candy inside the store but for less.
jokes.setup: Being asked to buy charity candy
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the economic irrationality of charity candy sales
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the economics of charity fundraising that many can relate to

jokes.text: Melts in your mouth, not in your pants.
jokes.setup: Selling candy with laxatives
jokes.punchline: Twist on M&M slogan referencing the laxative effects
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of famous advertising slogan with scatological twist that fits the plot

jokes.text: Holy moly. I'm moving like Ginger Rogers.
jokes.setup: Customer affected by laxative candy
jokes.punchline: Comparing bowel urgency to famous dancer's grace and speed
jokes.explanation: Creative comparison between laxative effects and dancing, with good cultural reference knowledge

jokes.text: Looks like this is one time the Indians didn't win.
jokes.setup: Pre-Teen Braves losing the candy contest
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's reference to historical pattern of Native American defeats
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor that's characteristic of Wiggum's inappropriate observations, though potentially insensitive

jokes.text: I'm gonna take that bartending course, including today's lighter drinks.
jokes.setup: Homer inspired by Bart's success
jokes.punchline: His focus on the alcohol aspect of bartending training
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer motivation - taking inspiration for wrong reasons and focusing on personal benefit

jokes.text: People will do anything a sign tells them.
jokes.setup: Using VIP parking with fake credentials
jokes.punchline: Homer's astute observation about human compliance with signage
jokes.explanation: Sharp social observation that's both funny and genuinely insightful about human behavior

jokes.text: Or did it?
jokes.setup: Homer drinking something to quench his thirst
jokes.punchline: Immediately questioning whether his thirst was actually quenched
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic overthinking of simple physical sensations in absurdist fashion

jokes.text: Do you have 5 bucks for regular parking? Because I don't.
jokes.setup: Father-son outing to baseball game
jokes.punchline: Father immediately reveals financial inadequacy to child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk Van Houten pathetic dad moment - immediately burdening his child with his failures. Creates appropriate cringe and character consistency.

jokes.text: Baseball? Eh.
jokes.setup: Announcer calls baseball America's favorite pastime
jokes.punchline: Homer's unenthusiastic response to supposed national passion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflation of American institutions. The casual dismissal of baseball as just 'eh' is economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You talk pretty tough for a man without health insurance. I'm on federal assistance.
jokes.setup: Fighting escalating between groups
jokes.punchline: Modern economic realities intruding on playground tough talk
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of playground bravado with adult economic concerns - very contemporary humor

jokes.text: I sang at your wedding. Yeah, 'The Best Is Yet to Come.' Real original.
jokes.setup: Adults fighting in the youth group brawl
jokes.punchline: Criticism of wedding song choice in the middle of a fight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor - petty personal grievances emerging during larger conflict

jokes.text: Then show us your boobs.
jokes.setup: Willie trying to comfort crying Marge
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate and crude suggestion
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate for Willie but relies on crude shock value without much craft or cleverness

jokes.text: Not a hymn to war like our national anthem... but a sweet soothing hymn like the national anthem of Canada.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they sing to end the violence
jokes.punchline: Calling the US national anthem warlike compared to Canada's peaceful one
jokes.explanation: Good international comparison humor highlighting different national attitudes through their anthems

jokes.text: Well, Bart, we've learned that war is not the answer. Except to all of America's problems. Amen.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to deliver moral lesson
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting the lesson with American foreign policy reality
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical ending that undercuts typical sitcom morality with political reality - sharp writing with great economy
